summary: Added a large THP decoder implementation by porting THP code/structure knowledge from Pikmin 2 into Melee's Dolphin external SDK area. The PR introduced extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c and extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/thp/thp.h, then renamed several previously anonymous THPDec_* symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to confident THP names. The PR body explains the central matching insight: other decompiled games use a later THP version with a global THPFileInfo pointer instead of passing THPFileInfo into each private function, and adapting to that global-pointer style was enough to match or near-match several functions.
what_changed: Created extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c with a large THP decoder implementation, including JPEG/THP table parsing, Huffman table generation, YUV decompression dispatch, GQR setup/restore helpers, inverse DCT-related code, and THPVideoDecode.
Created extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/thp/thp.h with THP typedefs and structs such as THPSample, THPCoeff, THPQuantTab, THPHuffmanTab, THPComponent, and THPFileInfo.
Added declarations for THP internals and public APIs including THPVideoDecode and THPInit.

Changed symbols.txt names for several THP functions from address-based placeholders to descriptive names, including THPVideoDecode, __THPReadFrameHeader, __THPReadQuantizationTable, __THPReadHuffmanTableSpecification, __THPReadScaneHeader, __THPHuffGenerateSizeTable, __THPHuffGenerateCodeTable, __THPHuffGenerateDecoderTables, and __THPRestartDefinition.
Kept some THPDec_8032xxxx names where the PR evidence only supported partial confidence or remaining unknowns.
smart_moves: Used another decompiled Nintendo title, Pikmin 2, as a source of THP implementation structure instead of trying to infer every THP routine from Melee in isolation.
Identified a versioning/ABI-style difference in THP implementations: later versions use a global THPFileInfo pointer rather than passing THPFileInfo to each private function.
Applied the global THPFileInfo pointer model through __THPInfo, which the PR body says was sufficient to match or near-match several functions.
Promoted only confidently identified functions in symbols.txt while leaving unresolved functions with address-based THPDec_* names.
Introduced a dedicated thp.h header to capture shared THP structs, typedefs, and function prototypes rather than embedding all layout knowledge only in the C file.
decomp_lessons: For SDK/library code, cross-game ports can reveal both symbol names and hidden implementation conventions, but matching may require adapting for library version differences rather than copying code verbatim.

A global context pointer can be a critical matching variable. If a candidate source from another game passes a struct through private functions but the target binary appears not to, test whether the target version stores that struct in a file-scope/global pointer.

Do not rename every unknown symbol just because a related source exists. This PR renamed functions whose behavior was confidently identified and retained placeholder THPDec_* names for less-certain functions.

Struct reconstruction for external SDK systems should encode observed offsets and padding explicitly when needed, as THPFileInfo uses padding fields and offset-sized arrays to line up with binary behavior.
Header additions are useful for decomp work when they collect reconstructed typedefs, structs, and static/public function declarations that are reused across a large source file.
Near-matching library ports can still be valuable because they unlock confident symbol names and establish the architecture of the subsystem.
naming_conventions: Public THP API names use the THP prefix without leading underscores, e.g. THPVideoDecode and THPInit.
Private/internal THP functions use a double-underscore THP prefix, e.g. __THPReadFrameHeader, __THPReadQuantizationTable, __THPHuffGenerateDecoderTables, and __THPRestartDefinition.
Specialized decompression routines include dimensions in the symbol name when known, e.g. __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448 and __THPDecompressiMCURow640x480.
Address-based placeholders use the form THPDec_8032F8D4 or THPDec_80331340 when no confident descriptive name is available.
THP struct/type names use PascalCase with the THP prefix, e.g. THPFileInfo, THPComponent, THPHuffmanTab, THPCoeff, and THPSample.
The header guard is THP_H and the C++ linkage wrapper uses extern "C".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Porting from Pikmin 2 was paired with a deliberate change from per-function THPFileInfo arguments to a global static THPFileInfo* __THPInfo to better match Melee's THP binary.
The implementation preserves low-level PowerPC/Gekko details such as GQR5/GQR6 setup and restore through inline assembly guarded by __MWERKS__.
Several globals are explicitly ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN(32), matching the alignment-sensitive nature of THP decoding buffers and GameCube cache/paired-single behavior.
The decoder uses specialized paths for known frame sizes, dispatching to __THPDecompressiMCURow512x448, __THPDecompressiMCURow640x480, or __THPDecompressiMCURowNxN.
The code uses exact-style control flow and labels in THPVideoDecode, including explicit error labels and comments like an empty branch being required for match.
Padding fields in THPFileInfo and related structs were used to preserve inferred layout rather than only semantic fields.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The only human explanation available is the PR body, which states that adapting later-version THP code to use a global THPFileInfo pointer was enough to match or near-match several functions and confidently name many symbols.
follow_up_queries: Search later THP PRs for remaining THPDec_8032xxxx or THPDec_8033xxxx placeholder names.
Check whether thp.h prototypes were later corrected or split between public and private declarations.
Look for follow-up matching work around __THPSetupBuffers, __THPHuffGenerateSizeTable, __THPHuffGenerateCodeTable, and remaining near-match functions.
Search for uses of __THPInfo and THPFileInfo layout changes after PR #1603.
Compare Melee THPDec.c against Pikmin 2 THP source to identify exact version differences that affected matching.
